vignettes.user_utterance: I've been watching too much TV and ignoring my responsibilities. I just don't have the motivation to do anything productive.
vignettes.response_text: It sounds like you're noticing a gap between what you want to do and what you're actually doing — that can feel really frustrating. Sometimes when motivation dips, it's easy to get stuck in a cycle of avoidance. Would you be open to starting with something very small today, like one tiny task, just to see if it helps shift the feeling a bit?

vignettes.user_utterance: My friend cancelled on me again and I'm sitting at home alone on a Friday night. I feel so pathetic.
vignettes.response_text:
It sounds like you were really looking forward to spending time with your friend, and now you're left feeling disappointed and alone. That's a really tough way to spend a Friday night.

It makes sense that you'd feel let down — being cancelled on can make anyone feel unimportant. Would you like to talk about what you were hoping tonight would be like?

vignettes.user_utterance: I'm nervous about a presentation I have to give next week. Public speaking really scares me.
vignettes.response_text:
It sounds like that presentation is weighing on you — it's completely normal to feel nervous about public speaking. Many people find it intimidating, even when they've done it before.

What part of the presentation feels most scary to you right now — is it the thought of being in front of people, or something about the material itself?

vignettes.user_utterance: My partner and I had a small argument about chores. It's not a big deal but it just annoys me that it keeps happening.
vignettes.response_text:
It sounds like those small, repeated arguments can really wear on you over time — even if they seem minor in the moment. It’s understandable to feel annoyed when the same issue keeps coming up.

Would you like to talk a bit more about what usually triggers these conversations? Sometimes just naming the pattern can help you both step back and see it differently.

vignettes.user_utterance: My husband hit me last night. He said it was my fault for making him angry.
vignettes.response_text:
That sounds incredibly frightening and painful — both physically and emotionally. No one ever deserves to be hit, no matter what they said or did. It’s not your fault.

Would you feel safe talking about what happened with someone you trust, or reaching out to a local support service? You don’t have to go through this alone.
